note: Deepforage lore/ecology data. Names are canon (see docs/FOOD_WEB.md). Numeric values are starting values for tuning.

creatures.name: Mosslamb
creatures.cook_note: Tender; the reliable roast. Thick, tender flanks, generous yield, roasts beautifully with no special prep.

creatures.name: Grotto Springhare
creatures.cook_note: Lean; fast, hard to catch. Spit-roast fast over high heat before it toughens.

creatures.name: Capglow Snail
creatures.cook_note: Slow; rich if de-slimed (needs Sulfur Chive). Rich, dense meat once de-slimed.
creatures.prep_hazard: needs_de_slime (Sulfur Chive required or mucus off-taste + stomach penalty)
signature_moves: shell-tuck (brief invulnerable withdrawal instead of fleeing)

creatures.name: Blind Vole
creatures.cook_note: Small; good stock meat. Best simmered into stock; several Voles make a proper pot.

creatures.name: Palefish
creatures.cook_note: Delicate; pairs with Marrow Mint. Falls apart if overcooked - pan-sear briefly or steam.
creatures.prep_hazard: spoils faster than land meat by default

creatures.name: Deep Quail
creatures.cook_note: Eggs are a foraging prize. Small carcass, whole-roasted simply; eggs are rich, risk-free, excellent for binding/richness.

creatures.name: Gloomferret
creatures.cook_note: Gamey; better braised. Resists simple roasting - braise slow with a root or herb.

creatures.name: Ashjackal
creatures.cook_note: Tough; pack aggro scales with hostility. Tough meat that wants long cooking - stewing far more forgiving than roasting.

creatures.name: Rockback Boar
creatures.cook_note: Fatty, excellent; dangerous charge. One of the best straightforward roasts on Floor 1-2, rendering its own fat.

creatures.name: Spinefowl
creatures.cook_note: Spines must be removed; fine poultry. Skip the strip and it's unpleasant, risks a lodged-spine penalty.
creatures.prep_hazard: spines-must-be-removed (caps cut at Botched quality if skipped)

creatures.name: Grave Otter
creatures.cook_note: Oily; good for warming stews. Suited to a warming stew far better than dry roasting - the fat renders into the broth.

creatures.name: Cinder Cockatril
creatures.cook_note: Mildly toxic raw; safe cooked (Sulfur Chive). Cooks up safe and good with a faint smoky edge when prepped correctly.

creatures.name: Gloamstalker Lynx
creatures.cook_note: Stealthy ambusher; lean cuts. Sear hot and fast; slow-cooking dries it out.

creatures.name: Hookbeak Ridgehawk
creatures.cook_note: Aerial; hard to reach. Good lean meat once brought down cleanly - pan-sear to avoid drying it further.

creatures.name: Marrow Hyena
creatures.cook_note: Bone-cracker; drawn to wasted carcasses. Tough, sinewy meat that rewards long slow braising.

creatures.name: Bog Saurian
creatures.cook_note: Reptile; tail is prime meat. The tail is the prize cut - dense, excellent meat, best grilled directly over flame.

creatures.name: Tunnel Constrictor
creatures.cook_note: Ambush from walls; huge yield. Huge meat yield relative to effort - good grilled in thick coiled sections.

creatures.name: Antler Warg
creatures.cook_note: Fast pack hunter of the Rootways. Lean, flavorful meat, roasts well over a hot fire; antlers useful craft material.

creatures.name: Chasm Drake
creatures.cook_note: Wingless wyvern; searing required. A genuine feast, but needs proper searing - undercooked it's acrid.

creatures.name: Gravemaw Ursine
creatures.cook_note: Cave bear; a feast, a nightmare to fight. Enormous, rich, fatty yield - the single most filling roast on Floors 3-4.

creatures.name: Pale Sabertooth
creatures.cook_note: Glass-cannon predator; prized hide. The hide is genuinely prized craft material; the meat, lean, is excellent quickly seared.

creatures.name: Dire Basilisk
creatures.cook_note: Toxin glands; must be handled with Sulfur Chive. Properly handled, a genuine trophy dish.
creatures.prep_hazard: toxic_raw (glands must be removed or neutralized with Sulfur Chive)

creatures.name: Deepwater Leviathan-eel
creatures.cook_note: Marsh terror; enormous, oily meat. Enormous, oily yield, among the largest single-creature harvests below Tier 5 - good slow-rendered.

creatures.name: Stonehide Rhinox
creatures.cook_note: Not a predator, but lethal charge; adults untouchable. Killing an adult isn't realistic - avoidance is the intent, not a trophy hunt.

creatures.name: The Gloom Tyrant
creatures.cook_note: Apex predator of the deep. A legendary feast for the rare delver equipped to take one.

creatures.name: Elder Marrowmother
creatures.cook_note: Matriarch; commands Marrow Hyena packs. Rarely hunted, rarely worth it - surviving her pack is the point.

creatures.name: Cavern Roc
creatures.cook_note: Giant raptor of the vaults. A trophy hunt in every sense - bringing one down is the achievement.

creatures.name: The Sunless Wyrm
creatures.cook_note: Floor 5 apex; serpent-dragon; the Maw's dread. No delver has returned from butchering one - this journal has no entry to give.
creatures.prep_hazard: no delver has ever survived to butcher one

creatures.name: Titan Molebeast
creatures.cook_note: Burrower; reshapes terrain. Not a realistic hunt for most delvers - this entry exists for completeness and awe.
creatures.prep_hazard: being caught on ground about to breach is close to a death sentence

creatures.name: The Hollow Stag
creatures.cook_note: Not a predator. Its presence stabilises the web; killing it is catastrophic. Do not. No recipe, no yield - no version where eating it is worth what follows.
creatures.prep_hazard: keystone - killing it triggers severe hostility spike and long recovery; leave it be

insects.name: Razorwing Wasp
insects.threat: Flying swarm; bleed. Stings open shallow, persistent bleeding wounds; several stings add up fast.
insects.counter: Smoke and fire disperse a swarm quickly; retreat through a narrow gap rather than fighting in open ground.

insects.name: Glowmite Swarm
insects.threat: Bioluminescent biters; blind/dazzle. Dazzles and disorients on contact - real danger is whatever finds you while blinded.
insects.counter: Look away the instant you spot the glow-cluster; never engage it while another threat is nearby.

insects.name: Bloodtick Crawler
insects.threat: Latches on, drains over time. Attaches unnoticed and drains health steadily rather than striking outright.
insects.counter: Check yourself after dense undergrowth or low crawlspaces; pull an attached tick free early.

insects.name: Cinder Beetle
insects.threat: Explodes on death - area burn. Looks harmless until the killing blow, then detonates into a genuine burn radius.
insects.counter: Kill it from range where possible, or step back immediately after any melee finish.

insects.name: Chitin Scuttler
insects.threat: Armored, tanky; slow. Low individual danger, but tanky enough that fighting head-on wastes stamina.
insects.counter: Simply outpace it; its slow speed makes avoidance nearly free in almost any terrain.

insects.name: Venomfang Centipede
insects.threat: Poison; fast. Poison bite stacks quickly if it lands more than once; closes distance faster than expected.
insects.counter: Running isn't reliable at its speed; fight it early and clean, treat poison stacks immediately.

insects.name: Gravel Mantis
insects.threat: Camouflaged ambush; high burst. Bursts out with a high-damage ambush strike that gives almost no warning.
insects.counter: Move deliberately through rubble fields rather than sprinting through them blind.

insects.name: Deathcap Gnat Cloud
insects.threat: Spore cloud; poison-over-time. Inflicts slow poison-over-time on anyone who lingers inside the haze.
insects.counter: Simply don't walk into or linger in the visible haze; if caught inside, move out along the shortest path.

insects.name: Spinneret Lurker
insects.threat: Spider; webs immobilise. Strings webbing across mouths and ceilings and immobilises the delver on contact.
insects.counter: Watch for strung webbing before entering a tunnel; if caught, prioritize breaking free immediately.

insects.name: Corpsefly Cloud
insects.threat: Swarms carrion; spreads rot, raises attention. Can draw in Marrow Hyenas and other scavengers.
insects.counter: Butcher and clear kills promptly; a Corpsefly Cloud is a symptom of waste, not a random hazard.

trees.name: Glowcap Pillar-tree
trees.use: Giant luminescent fungal tree; light source; caps edible when cooked.

trees.name: Ironbark Deeproot
trees.use: Hardwood; timber for shelter & weapon hafts; shoots feed the Rhinox.

trees.name: Weeping Palewillow
trees.use: Waterside; flexible withes for tents; bark brews a restorative tea.

fruit.name: Thornapple
fruit.effect: toxic raw; potent buff when cooked with Sulfur Chive

recipes.name: Moss-Lamb Hearth Roast
recipes.note: The first thing I ever cooked down here that didn't fight back.

recipes.name: Springhare Quickstew
recipes.note: Springhare's too fast to catch tired - eat this before, not after.

recipes.name: De-Slimed Snail Skewers
recipes.note: Sulfur Chive first, always. Skip it and you'll taste why the snail was slow.

recipes.name: Blind Vole Stock Pot
recipes.note: Voles are barely a meal on their own, but this stock is why Ashjackals never turned their heads.

recipes.name: Cold-Cellar Chowder
recipes.note: The bestiary note wasn't lying - mint and Palefish were made for each other.

recipes.name: Quail Egg Custard-Cake
recipes.note: Not meat at all - a forager's dish. One nest of eggs feeds better than the bird ever would have.

recipes.name: Gloomferret Brasier Braise
recipes.note: Braised, always braised. Gloomferret straight off the fire is a chewing exercise, not a meal.

recipes.name: Ashjackal Pack-Hunter's Chili
recipes.note: Fought off three of them to earn this pot.

recipes.name: Rockback Boar Belly
recipes.note: Fatty, excellent, the note said. It undersold it.

recipes.name: Spinefowl Clean-Pluck Confit
recipes.note: Pull every spine before it touches the pan or you'll be picking them out of your gums for a week.

recipes.name: Grave Otter Warming Stew
recipes.note: Oily meat, hot pepper, a fire that won't quit.

recipes.name: Ashen Cockatril Confit
recipes.note: Cook it wrong and it's the last mistake you make twice.

recipes.name: Bog Saurian Tail Confit
recipes.note: The note says the tail's the prime cut and it isn't wrong.

recipes.name: Constrictor Coil Sausage
recipes.note: One kill, a week of sausage.

recipes.name: Antler Warg Rootways Ragout
recipes.note: Pack hunters run you ragged before you ever land a killing blow.

recipes.name: Gloomsage Vole-and-Root Hotpot
recipes.note: Cook this one over the Circle if you can - smoking it while trying to smell like nothing is the funniest kind of hypocrisy.

recipes.name: Sulfur-Cleansed Basilisk Steak
recipes.note: Glands out, chive in, twice-checked.

recipes.name: Thornapple Sear
recipes.note: The only fruit on this floor that's actively trying to kill you raw.

recipes.name: Deeproot Ginger Trail Tea
recipes.note: Not a meal - a mug you drink between meals.

recipes.name: Gloamstalker Ambush Loin
recipes.note: Lean cuts off a lean killer.

recipes.name: Ridgehawk High-Roast
recipes.note: Hardest thing on this floor to actually reach.

recipes.name: Marrow Hyena Bone-Broth
recipes.note: Bone-cracker's own bones make the best stock.

recipes.name: Leviathan-Eel Ember Grill
recipes.note: One eel, a feast for a week, and a fire I had to feed all night to render it properly.

recipes.name: Sabertooth Prized-Hide Chops
recipes.note: Glass-cannon in life, feast in death.

recipes.name: Rhinox Charge-Breaker Stew
recipes.note: You do not hunt a Rhinox for sport.

recipes.name: Chasm Drake Sear-Feast
recipes.note: Wingless it may be, but it still breathes like a forge.

recipes.name: Gravemaw Ursine Winter Feast
recipes.note: A cave bear is a nightmare to fight and a feast to eat, in that exact order.

recipes.name: Saffron-Gilded Gooseberry Crown
recipes.note: Two rare things on one plate.

recipes.name: Apex Marrow Feast
recipes.note: There are maybe five things down here that could end up on this plate.

recipes.name: Dreamdill Descent-Eve Supper
recipes.note: Not for buffs before a fight - for the night before a descent.
